folder_role: Test fixture directory for evidence marker fence edge cases, providing markdown samples that test the robustness of [EVIDENCE:...] marker parsing against various fence block configurations.
architecture_observation: fixture-only - contains 3 markdown fixture files that test edge cases in evidence marker parsing within fenced code blocks without any executable code or subdirectories.

public_entrypoints.name: evidence-indented-fence.md
public_entrypoints.purpose: Fixture testing that evidence markers inside indented fenced code blocks with paren-heavy content are correctly ignored.

public_entrypoints.name: evidence-mismatched-fence.md
public_entrypoints.purpose: Fixture testing the documented false-negative behavior for mismatched fence blocks (unclosed fences).

public_entrypoints.name: evidence-nested-fence.md
public_entrypoints.purpose: Fixture testing that inner triple-backtick lines inside an outer four-backtick fence are preserved, and markers after the outer fence closes are correctly parsed.

integration_notes: This folder provides test fixtures for the evidence marker audit system, which validates that the parseMarkers function correctly handles [EVIDENCE:...] markers in markdown files while respecting fenced code block boundaries. The fixtures cover three critical edge cases: indented fences (where leading whitespace can confuse fence detection), mismatched fences (where unclosed fence blocks create false-negative scenarios that are documented as acceptable behavior), and nested fences (where triple-backtick blocks inside four-backtick blocks should be treated as inner content, not as closing fences). These fixtures ensure the evidence marker parser maintains robustness against real-world markdown edge cases that can occur in spec documentation, preventing false positives where markers inside code blocks are incorrectly extracted, and maintaining documented behavior for known false-negative scenarios. The test suite validates that parseMarkers returns empty arrays for markers inside code contexts and correctly extracts markers that appear outside of fenced blocks.
key_invariants: Each fixture must be valid markdown with proper fence block syntax
Fixtures must test specific edge cases in fence parsing (indentation, mismatching, nesting)
The evidence-indented-fence.md fixture must test that markers inside indented triple-backtick blocks return empty results
The evidence-mismatched-fence.md fixture must test the documented false-negative behavior for unclosed fences
The evidence-nested-fence.md fixture must test that inner triple-backticks inside outer four-backticks are preserved as content
Fixtures must be loadable by the loadFixture() helper function in the test file
Fixture filenames must match the pattern evidence-{edge-case-type}.md
Fixtures must include [EVIDENCE:...] markers to test parsing behavior
Fixtures must represent realistic markdown edge cases that can occur in spec documentation
The parseMarkers function must handle these fixtures according to the documented fence parsing rules
